dispositions.rationale: The AO title 'MAJORITY PARTNER' (LLP), the provider surname BRUCK differing from owner FUNK, and web confirmation (own site 'meet-the-doctors' + Yelp) that the single Shorewood office is staffed by 3 dentists (Bruck, Funk, Vicioso) make this a multi-dentist single-location group, not a solo T1. It is clearly dentist-owned (Funk documented owner since 1974, provider_count 3 < 8, no DSO brand), so correct to single_loc_group. The shared_ein and shared_phone each reach only one other location_id and the practice is verified single-site, so those are duplicate/variant rows rather than a real multi-office network.

Both the shared_ein (362701991) and the shared_website_domain (awesomebraces.com) point to the SAME second location_id (71ca73b0ba4aa992), and the researcher's own reasoning lists two addresses (507 S La Grange Rd and 47 S 6th Ave Ste F). A shared tax EIN is dispositive of common ownership, and the owner/provider is the same person (Bronke Wind), so this is her own dentist-owned two-office group (rule 6a), not a solo T1. Correct to dentist_multi at medium confidence since the second office is corroborated by the EIN/domain match rather than independently deep-verified.

The db_corporate_conflict (hard) tags this as dso_national / 'Midwest Dental' (pe_sponsor Gryphon), and Midwest Dental is on the protected known-DSO brand list. Per binding rules 2 and 3, a known DSO brand appearing as T1 can never be accepted on the strength of an own-site 'privately owned' statement (DSOs routinely keep the acquired local name); it must go to the adversarial verifier. Holding for DSO verification rather than promoting or accepting.

Web search refutes the researcher's premise that this is 'led by Dr. Scott Vlk': Vlk was FORCED TO SELL the practice, and the current NPPES AO is Molly Walsh, a non-clinical 'Director of Operations' (hard signal) who reaches 2 location_ids. An undisclosed post-sale acquirer plus a non-clinical operations executive spanning multiple offices is a classic group/MSO back-office pattern (rule 6c) that cannot be cleared as an independent single-location group. Holding for adversarial DSO/group verification.

The db_corporate_conflict (hard) is 'Western Dental' (Sonrava), which is on the protected known-DSO brand list. Even though the researcher's collision argument (named for Western Ave, own domain, Sonrava has no IL locations) is plausible, binding rule 3 forbids accepting a known-DSO-brand name collision at T1/T2 and rule 2 routes a known-brand corporate conflict to the verifier. Holding for adversarial verification rather than accepting.

The db_corporate_conflict brand 'Comfort Dental' is NOT on the protected known-DSO list, and the researcher documented that the national Comfort Dental franchise operates in ~10 states with no IL locations and no locator entry for this Armitage Ave address, so the label is a debunked geographic-impossibility name collision (rule 2, accept branch). The AO is Dr. Angelo Stamos DMD titled PRESIDENT of the LLC (owner-dentist positive signal) and opencare confirms him practicing there. The db 'comfortdental.com' website is a name-match artifact of the national chain, not this office. Evidence leans directory-based, so confidence correctly stays medium (rule 5).

The db_corporate_conflict is dso_national with NO brand (affiliated_dso null), and the researcher's own-site fetch shows a specific independent-ownership narrative: family-owned, founded 2003 by Dr. Mark Morris, continued by Drs. Arora and Mody after his 2024 passing, 8 named dentists across 2 owned locations. The shared_phone reaching one other location is the group's own second office, and provider_count 11 is consistent with a 2-location multi-specialty group (rule 1's high-count hold applies to T1/T2, not this correctly-assigned T3). Own-site family ownership rebuts the brand-less stale detector label; accept dentist_multi.

Web search plus BBB/Yelp/CareCredit/WebMD consistently show the single 10142 S Western Ave office is dentist-led by Dr. Laila Salman DDS and Dr. Daniel Smulson DDS (a known independent Chicago periodontist), a multi-provider single-location group with no corporate/DSO brand anywhere. The AO Kristine Kowalski is explicitly an OFFICE MANAGER (benign non-owner covering related offices, rule 6b), and the shared_ein reaching one other location_id shows no DSO brand or MSO signal. Evidence is directory-weighted so confidence stays at medium (rule 5); the assigned single_loc_group is the correct conservative independent tier.

The researcher themselves flagged the unresolved signal: this NPI's EIN (201771535) is shared with a same-unit Dental 360 / Family Dental Group entity, and Dental 360/Brite is on the protected known-DSO list. Compounding it, a shared Data-Axle officer (RIZKALLA, GEORGE) reaches 3 location_ids and the shared domain abrushabove.net reaches another location. A shared corporate EIN cluster tied to a known DSO brand plus a common officer across 3 offices is MSO back-office infrastructure (rules 3 and 6c) that must not merge as an independent group. Holding for adversarial DSO verification.

As with the other Comfort Dental collision this batch, the brand is NOT on the protected known-DSO list and the national franchise has no IL presence; the own website carries a positive independent-ownership statement (the dentist 'fulfilled her lifelong dream of owning her own dental practice') and search notes it 'appears to be a separate entity from the larger Comfort Dental franchise network' (rule 2, accept branch). The 'Partnership' name plus Yelp's 2 physicians at one address support the single_loc_group tier; the Relias-to-Rhie ownership change is between two independent dentists with no corporate signal. Evidence is directory/own-site so confidence correctly held at medium (rule 5).
